trade.summary: Brasile exports beef, soybeans, coffee, and aircraft to the Regno Unito. The Regno Unito exports machinery, pharmaceuticals, and financial services. Post-Brexit, the Regno Unito has sought to deepen bilateral trade ties independently of the EU.

diplomatic.summary: Historically warm relations. The Regno Unito and Brasile cooperate on climate issues, with the Regno Unito having contributed to Amazon Fund. Post-Brexit Regno Unito has sought a bilateral trade agreement with Brasile/Mercosur. Both are G20 members.

military.summary: No formal alliance. Historical ties from colonial era. Some defense cooperation and equipment sales. Limited joint training activities.

financial.summary: London is a major financial hub for Brasileian companies. HSBC, Standard Chartered, and other British banks operate in Brasile. British FDI in oil (Shell), mining, and financial services is significant.

energy.summary: Shell has significant operations in Brasileian offshore oil fields. BP is also active. Energy investment is a key dimension of the bilateral relationship.

scientific.summary: Strong academic ties. Regno Unito universities partner with Brasileian institutions. Newton Fund supported bilateral research. Collaboration in tropical medicine, climate, and biodiversity.
